title: String and text processing deterministic draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and runnable core task for String and text processing; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: String and text processing: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? The == operator compares String text, and one char always represents one complete Unicode character.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Use equals for String content and code-point-aware operations when Unicode characters matter; reserve == for reference identity checks.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Use equals for String content and code-point-aware operations when Unicode characters matter; reserve == for reference identity checks.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Use equals for String content and code-point-aware operations when Unicode characters matter; reserve == for reference identity checks.
The == operator compares String text, and one char always represents one complete Unicode character.

answer.explanation: Use equals for String content and code-point-aware operations when Unicode characters matter; reserve == for reference identity checks.

items.title: String and text processing: runnable core task
items.prompt: Read two complete lines. Print equal or different using String content equality, then print their combined Unicode code-point count.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Start from the stated input/output contract, implement the smallest language-specific operation, and run the visible boundary before changing structure.

feedback.correct: The visible and hidden Java: Beginner to Intermediate runner checks match the declared contract.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic runner check differs; inspect input parsing, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Compiles or parses and passes every visible and hidden deterministic runner check for the declared contract.

privateAuthorNotes: Reference solution and both tests are AI-assisted draft material. Execute in the pinned runner and obtain independent human review before any exam use.

answer.explanation: The task uses equals for content and codePointCount rather than reference identity or raw UTF-16 length.
